changed_functions.name: SmartlockerConstructOriginClaim (sub_1C001F138)
key_changes: Unpatched version allocates a buffer via ExAllocatePoolWithTag(PagedPool, size, 'smtA') WITHOUT zeroing it afterward, leaving 4 bytes uninitialized when arg5==NULL (bytes 0x3C-0x3F) and roughly 6 trailing bytes when arg5!=NULL

Patched version adds a memset(buf, 0, size) that zeroes the whole buffer, but only on the code path taken when Feature_965997883__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage @ 0x1C0002194 returns non-zero; the feature-disabled path (0x1C001F20B) allocates and jumps past the memset to 0x1C001F1C7, identical to the unpatched behavior (staged rollout)
For the arg5!=NULL case the size base becomes *arg5 + 0x40 when the feature is enabled and *arg5 + 0x42 (the original value) when it is disabled

The buffer is partially initialized (fields at 0x00-0x0C, BCryptGenRandom at 0x10-0x2F, metadata at 0x30-0x37, conditional 0x38+); the trailing bytes past the last written field retain stale kernel pool contents in the unpatched build

Buffer is returned via *arg7 (ptr) and *arg6 (full size) and is carried at full length into a process-token security attribute (SeSetSecurityAttributesToken via SmartlockerTagProcessToken @ 0x1C001F0C0) and a file extended attribute (AipSetEaFromOrigin)
changed_functions.security_notes: Use of uninitialized resource (CWE-908) with a small information-disclosure consequence (CWE-200). The unpatched ExAllocatePoolWithTag returns uninitialized paged pool memory; only specific fields are written, leaving 4 bytes (arg5==NULL) or ~6 trailing bytes (arg5!=NULL) uninitialized. Both callers (SmartlockerManagedInstallerCheck @ 0x1C001F25C, SmartlockerSmartscreenProcessTokenCheck @ 0x1C001F374) pass the buffer at its full length to SmartlockerTagProcessToken @ 0x1C001F0C0, which wraps it as a CLAIM_SECURITY_ATTRIBUTE OCTET_STRING and calls SeSetSecurityAttributesToken (0x1C001F11F); SmartlockerManagedInstallerCheck also returns it up to SmartlockerVerifyProcess @ 0x1C0020344, which writes it into a file extended attribute via AipSetEaFromOrigin. Token security attributes and file EAs are user-mode-queryable by Windows design, but that read is performed by the OS, not by this driver, and the disclosed quantity is only 4-6 bytes of pool residue. The patched build adds a memset that zeroes the buffer, but only when Feature_965997883__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage is enabled; the feature-disabled path retains the original unzeroed allocation. This is a feature-staged rollout, not an unconditional fix. Claims of a KASLR bypass or token/EPROCESS harvest are not supported by the binaries.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
SmartlockerConstructOriginClaim @ 0x1C001F138 (UNPATCHED):
00000001C001F138  mov     [rsp+arg_0], rbx
00000001C001F13D  mov     [rsp+arg_8], rbp
00000001C001F142  mov     [rsp+arg_10], rsi
00000001C001F147  push    rdi
00000001C001F148  push    r14
00000001C001F14A  push    r15
00000001C001F14C  sub     rsp, 20h
00000001C001F150  mov     rdi, [rsp+38h+arg_20]   ; arg5
00000001C001F155  mov     r15d, r9d               ; arg4
00000001C001F158  mov     ebp, ecx                ; arg1
00000001C001F15A  mov     esi, 40h                ; default size = 0x40
00000001C001F15F  test    rdi, rdi
00000001C001F162  jz      short loc_1C001F16A
00000001C001F164  movzx   esi, word ptr [rdi]     ; size = *arg5
00000001C001F167  add     esi, 42h                ; size += 0x42 (no zeroing follows)
00000001C001F16A  mov     r14d, 1
00000001C001F170  cmp     ebp, r14d
00000001C001F173  jbe     short loc_1C001F17F
00000001C001F175  mov     eax, 0C000000Dh         ; STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER
00000001C001F17A  jmp     loc_1C001F23A
00000001C001F17F  mov     edx, esi                ; NumberOfBytes
00000001C001F181  mov     r8d, 41746D73h          ; Tag 'smtA'
00000001C001F187  mov     ecx, r14d               ; PoolType = PagedPool
00000001C001F18A  call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
00000001C001F191  nop     dword ptr [rax+rax+00h]
00000001C001F196  mov     rbx, rax
00000001C001F199  test    rax, rax
00000001C001F19C  jnz     short loc_1C001F1A8
00000001C001F19E  mov     eax, 0C0000017h         ; STATUS_NO_MEMORY
00000001C001F1A3  jmp     loc_1C001F23A
00000001C001F1A8  and     dword ptr [rax+8], 0    ; buf[0x08]=0
00000001C001F1AC  lea     rdx, [rax+10h]
00000001C001F1B0  mov     r9d, 2
00000001C001F1B6  mov     [rax], r14d             ; buf[0x00]=1
00000001C001F1B9  xor     ecx, ecx
00000001C001F1BB  mov     [rax+4], ebp            ; buf[0x04]=arg1
00000001C001F1BE  mov     [rax+0Ch], r14d         ; buf[0x0C]=1
00000001C001F1C2  lea     r8d, [r9+0Eh]
00000001C001F1C6  call    cs:__imp_BCryptGenRandom ; buf[0x10..0x1F]
00000001C001F1CD  nop     dword ptr [rax+rax+00h]
00000001C001F1D2  lea     rdx, [rbx+20h]
00000001C001F1D6  test    ebp, ebp
00000001C001F1D8  jnz     short loc_1C001F1E4
00000001C001F1DA  movups  xmm0, xmmword ptr [rbx+10h]
00000001C001F1DE  movdqu  xmmword ptr [rdx], xmm0 ; buf[0x20..0x2F]=buf[0x10..0x1F]
00000001C001F1E2  jmp     short loc_1C001F1FA
00000001C001F1E4  xor     ecx, ecx
00000001C001F1E6  lea     r9d, [rcx+2]
00000001C001F1EA  lea     r8d, [rcx+10h]
00000001C001F1EE  call    cs:__imp_BCryptGenRandom ; buf[0x20..0x2F]
00000001C001F1F5  nop     dword ptr [rax+rax+00h]
00000001C001F1FA  mov     [rbx+30h], r15d         ; buf[0x30]=arg4
00000001C001F1FE  mov     eax, cs:dword_1C0016660 ; version
00000001C001F204  mov     [rbx+34h], eax          ; buf[0x34]=version
00000001C001F207  test    rdi, rdi
00000001C001F20A  jz      short loc_1C001F225
00000001C001F20C  movzx   eax, word ptr [rdi]
00000001C001F20F  lea     rcx, [rbx+3Ch]
00000001C001F213  mov     [rbx+38h], eax          ; buf[0x38]=*arg5
00000001C001F216  movzx   r8d, word ptr [rdi]     ; Size
00000001C001F21A  mov     rdx, [rdi+8]            ; Src
00000001C001F21E  call    memmove                 ; copy *arg5 bytes to buf[0x3C]
00000001C001F223  jmp     short loc_1C001F229
00000001C001F225  and     dword ptr [rbx+38h], 0  ; arg5==NULL: buf[0x38]=0, buf[0x3C..0x3F] left uninitialized
00000001C001F229  mov     rax, [rsp+38h+arg_28]   ; arg6
00000001C001F22E  mov     [rax], esi              ; *arg6=size
00000001C001F230  mov     rax, [rsp+38h+arg_30]   ; arg7
00000001C001F235  mov     [rax], rbx              ; *arg7=buffer
00000001C001F238  xor     eax, eax                ; STATUS_SUCCESS
00000001C001F23A  mov     rbx, [rsp+38h+arg_0]
00000001C001F23F  mov     rbp, [rsp+38h+arg_8]
00000001C001F244  mov     rsi, [rsp+38h+arg_10]
00000001C001F249  add     rsp, 20h
00000001C001F24D  pop     r15
00000001C001F24F  pop     r14
00000001C001F251  pop     rdi
00000001C001F252  retn

PATCHED @ 0x1C001F138 (allocation region, feature-gated):
00000001C001F165  call    Feature_965997883__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
00000001C001F174  add     esi, 40h                ; size += 0x40
00000001C001F177  test    edx, edx
00000001C001F179  jnz     short loc_1C001F17E     ; feature ON -> keep +0x40
00000001C001F17B  add     esi, 2                  ; feature OFF -> +0x42 (original)
00000001C001F190  call    Feature_965997883__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
00000001C001F1A2  test    eax, eax
00000001C001F1A4  jz      short loc_1C001F20B     ; feature OFF -> allocate WITHOUT zeroing
00000001C001F1A6  call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
00000001C001F1BF  mov     rcx, rax
00000001C001F1C2  call    memset                  ; THE FIX (feature ON path only)
00000001C001F20B  call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag ; feature OFF path
00000001C001F21D  jnz     short loc_1C001F1C7     ; jumps PAST the memset

changed_functions.name: Feature_4060024122__private_IsEnabledFallback (sub_1C0002E00)
key_changes: Thunk function: unpatched calls wil_details_IsEnabledFallback (sub_1c0002850)(arg1, arg2); patched (relocated to 0x1C0002E74) calls wil_details_IsEnabledFallback (sub_1c0001bb0)(arg1, arg2, &Feature_4060024122__private_descriptor) — adds a third parameter (this feature's descriptor) so the callee stops relying on the single global descriptor
changed_functions.security_notes: Not a security change. Part of a WIL (Windows Implementation Library) feature-staging refactor: the callee stops hard-coding the single global feature descriptor and instead receives a per-descriptor context pointer as a parameter. The underlying feature-state access was already a correct lock-free atomic compare-and-swap in both builds, and none of these functions participate in an AppLocker allow/deny decision.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
--- UNPATCHED Feature_4060024122__private_IsEnabledFallback (sub_1C0002E00)
sub     rsp, 0x28
call    0x1c0002850              ; 2 args
add     rsp, 0x28
retn
+++ PATCHED Feature_4060024122__private_IsEnabledFallback (sub_1C0002E74)
sub     rsp, 0x28
lea     r8, [rel 0x1c0015b70]    ; 3rd arg = context ptr
call    0x1c0001bb0              ; 3 args
add     rsp, 0x28
retn

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureStateCache_TryEnableDeviceUsageFastPath (sub_1C00028DC)
key_changes: Unpatched: atomic compare-and-swap targets the global feature descriptor (wil_details_featureDescriptors_a, loaded at 0x1C00028DC)
Patched (relocated to 0x1C0001C44): takes a new third parameter arg3 (per-descriptor context pointer); the same CAS targets *arg3, with added checks on per-descriptor bytes at arg3+0x1d and arg3+0x1e
Patched adds a lock or fast path (0x1C0001C89) when *(arg3+0x1e) or *(arg3+0x1d) is set, instead of the cmpxchg loop
changed_functions.security_notes: Not a security change. The unpatched code at 0x1C00028DC performs a correct lock-free atomic compare-and-swap: it loads the current value (0x1C00028F9), computes the new value, does lock cmpxchg [r9], edx (0x1C000290E), and on failure jnz (0x1C0002913) branches back to 0x1C00028FC to re-read and retry. That is a thread-safe CAS-with-retry loop in both builds, so there is no genuine data race and no torn or lost update. The patch only changes where the descriptor comes from (global vs. *arg3), a WIL feature-staging refactor. These bits are WIL feature enablement/usage telemetry; no AppLocker allow/deny decision is involved. CWE-362/CWE-366 do not apply.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED wil_details_FeatureStateCache_TryEnableDeviceUsageFastPath @ 0x1C00028DC:
00000001C00028DC  mov     r9, cs:wil_details_featureDescriptors_a  ; global descriptor
00000001C00028E3  sub     edx, 3
00000001C00028E6  jz      short loc_1C00028F3
00000001C00028E8  cmp     edx, 1
00000001C00028EB  jnz     short locret_1C0002915
00000001C00028ED  lea     r8d, [rdx+1Fh]          ; 0x20 for event type 4
00000001C00028F1  jmp     short loc_1C00028F9
00000001C00028F3  mov     r8d, 10h                ; 0x10 for event type 3
00000001C00028F9  mov     eax, [r9]               ; read state (re-read each retry)
00000001C00028FC  test    al, 2
00000001C00028FE  jz      short locret_1C0002915
00000001C0002900  mov     edx, eax
00000001C0002902  xor     edx, ecx
00000001C0002904  test    dl, 1
00000001C0002907  jnz     short locret_1C0002915
00000001C0002909  mov     edx, eax
00000001C000290B  or      edx, r8d
00000001C000290E  lock cmpxchg [r9], edx          ; atomic CAS
00000001C0002913  jnz     short loc_1C00028FC      ; retry -> correct lock-free loop
00000001C0002915  retn

PATCHED @ 0x1C0001C44 (same CAS on *arg3):
00000001C0001C44  mov     r9, [r8]                 ; per-descriptor state = *arg3
00000001C0001C5D  cmp     byte ptr [r8+1Eh], 0
00000001C0001C62  jnz     short loc_1C0001C89
00000001C0001C64  cmp     byte ptr [r8+1Dh], 0
00000001C0001C69  jnz     short loc_1C0001C89
00000001C0001C80  lock cmpxchg [r9], edx
00000001C0001C87  jmp     short loc_1C0001C6E      ; retry
00000001C0001C89  lock or [r9], r10d               ; fast path

changed_functions.name: wil_details_IsEnabledFallback (sub_1C0002850)
key_changes: Unpatched: hard-codes the global descriptor (mov rcx, cs:wil_details_featureDescriptors_a at 0x1C0002880) when calling wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState

Patched (relocated to 0x1C0001BB0): takes new arg3 (descriptor pointer); reads the descriptor from *arg3 (mov rcx, [r8] at 0x1C0001BDD) and threads arg3 down to the usage-reporting and fast-path helpers
changed_functions.security_notes: Not a security change. This orchestrator drives the WIL feature-enablement state cache. The refactor moves it from the single global feature descriptor to a per-descriptor context passed as arg3. There is no synchronization defect: the underlying state update is a correct lock-free atomic CAS in both builds, and none of this participates in an AppLocker allow/deny decision.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED wil_details_IsEnabledFallback @ 0x1C0002850 (excerpt):
00000001C0002879  lea     r8, wil_details_featureDescriptors_a
00000001C0002880  mov     rcx, cs:wil_details_featureDescriptors_a  ; hard-coded global
00000001C0002887  call    wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState
00000001C00028A9  call    wil_details_FeatureReporting_ReportUsageToService
00000001C00028BB  call    wil_details_FeatureStateCache_TryEnableDeviceUsageFastPath

PATCHED @ 0x1C0001BB0 (excerpt):
00000001C0001BCA  mov     r14, r8                  ; save arg3
00000001C0001BDD  mov     rcx, [r8]                ; descriptor = *arg3
00000001C0001BE0  call    wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState
00000001C0001C02  mov     rcx, r14
00000001C0001C05  call    wil_details_FeatureReporting_ReportUsageToService
00000001C0001C12  mov     r8, r14
00000001C0001C1A  call    wil_details_FeatureStateCache_TryEnableDeviceUsageFastPath

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureReporting_ReportUsageToServiceDirect (sub_1C00022FC)
key_changes: Unpatched: uses hardcoded feature GUID 0x39f21e2 and global config table data_1c0016208 for RtlQueryFeatureConfiguration and RtlNotifyFeatureUsage
Patched: takes new arg1 (context pointer); reads feature GUID from *(arg1+0x18) and config table from *(arg1+8) — supports per-instance feature configuration

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureReporting_ReportUsageToService (sub_1C00023EC)
key_changes: Unpatched: uses hardcoded GUID 0x39f21e2 and global data_1c0015b68 for feature event dispatch; passes hardcoded values to callback via data_1c0016228
Patched: takes new arg1 (context pointer); reads GUID from *(arg1+0x18), config from *(arg1+0x10), and callback from data_1c0016230 — per-instance configuration support
Otherwise functionally identical (same event type mapping logic, same dispatch pattern)

security_findings.entry_point: AppLocker policy evaluation reachable through file execution/creation operations that trigger AppID/Smartlocker evaluation
call_chain: file execution/creation on an AppLocker/WDAC-enforced system
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Use of Uninitialized Resource (CWE-908); information-disclosure consequence (CWE-200)
security_findings.description: The function SmartlockerConstructOriginClaim (sub_1C001F138) allocates a kernel paged pool buffer via ExAllocatePoolWithTag(PagedPool, size, 'smtA') but does NOT zero it before partially initializing it. Fields written: [0x00]=1, [0x04]=arg1, [0x08]=0, [0x0C]=1, [0x10-0x1F]=BCryptGenRandom, [0x20-0x2F]=BCryptGenRandom or a copy of [0x10-0x1F], [0x30-0x33]=arg4, [0x34-0x37]=version, [0x38+]=conditional. When arg5==NULL the allocation is 0x40 bytes and bytes 0x3C-0x3F (4 bytes) are never written; when arg5!=NULL the allocation is *arg5+0x42 bytes and roughly 6 trailing bytes are left uninitialized. Those bytes retain stale paged pool residue. The patched build adds memset(buf, 0, size) to scrub the buffer, but only on the path taken when Feature_965997883__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage @ 0x1C0002194 returns non-zero; the feature-disabled path allocates without zeroing, identical to the unpatched code. The fix is therefore a WIL feature-staged rollout, not an unconditional fix.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Triggering requires an AppLocker/Smartlocker policy evaluation path that causes SmartlockerConstructOriginClaim to allocate a claim buffer, during file execution/creation on an AppLocker/WDAC-enforced system. The callers SmartlockerManagedInstallerCheck (sub_1C001F25C) and SmartlockerSmartscreenProcessTokenCheck (sub_1C001F374) are reached from SmartlockerVerifyProcess (sub_1c0020344). The optional arg5 (at rsp+0x60, a struct with a WORD size at offset 0 and a data pointer at offset 8) controls the allocation size: *arg5+0x42 when provided (~6 uninitialized trailing bytes), or 0x40 when NULL (bytes 0x3C-0x3F uninitialized).
security_findings.primitive: Disclosure of 4-6 bytes of uninitialized paged pool residue per allocation. The buffer, at full length, is carried into a process-token security attribute (SeSetSecurityAttributesToken via SmartlockerTagProcessToken @ 0x1C001F0C0) and a file extended attribute (AipSetEaFromOrigin); both object types are user-mode-queryable by Windows design, though the actual read is performed by the OS rather than by this driver, and the driver itself does not copy the buffer to any user IRP/IOCTL output buffer. The disassembly does not establish that the leaked bytes contain any specific class of sensitive data; claims of KASLR bypass or token/EPROCESS harvest are not supported.

breakpoints.why: The call cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag in the unpatched build. At this point: ecx=PagedPool(1), edx=allocation size (rsi, either 0x40 or *arg5+0x42), r8d=tag 'smtA'. After this call returns, rax holds the allocated but unzeroed buffer pointer. In the patched build this call is on the feature-enabled path at 0x1C001F1A6 and is followed by a memset at 0x1C001F1C2.

This is the success return path where *arg6 = size and *arg7 = buffer_ptr. At this point rbx holds the buffer pointer. Inspect rbx[0x3C..0x3F] (when arg5==NULL) or trailing bytes to confirm uninitialized/stale data is present in the buffer being returned.

watch: rbx (after 0x1c001f196) = allocated buffer pointer — inspect bytes at offsets 0x3C-0x3F and beyond the last initialized field for stale pool residue
esi (at 0x1c001f17f) = allocation size — 0x40 for default path, *arg5+0x42 when arg5 is provided
rdi (at 0x1c001f150) = arg5 pointer (from rsp+0x60) — if NULL, fields 0x3C+ are uninitialized; if non-NULL, size and copy source come from this structure
qword [rsp+0x68] = arg6 (output size pointer), qword [rsp+0x70] = arg7 (output buffer pointer) — these receive the unzeroed buffer
key_offsets: 0x1C001F18A: call cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag — the allocation point; no zeroing follows in the unpatched build
0x1C001F1A8-0x1C001F204: partial field initialization — only specific dwords/qwords are written, leaving gaps
0x1C001F225: and dword ptr [rbx+38h], 0 — only zeroes offset 0x38 when arg5==NULL; bytes 0x3C-0x3F remain uninitialized
0x1C001F22E: mov [rax], esi — writes the allocation size to *arg6
0x1C001F235: mov [rax], rbx — writes the buffer pointer to *arg7
PATCHED 0x1C001F1C2: call memset — the zeroing, only on the feature-enabled path (0x1C001F1A6); the feature-disabled path (0x1C001F20B) skips it via jnz to 0x1C001F1C7
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: On a system with AppLocker/WDAC enabled, trigger a file execution or creation event that causes the AppID driver to evaluate policy and allocate a claim buffer. The call chain is: SmartlockerVerifyProcess (sub_1c0020344) → SmartlockerManagedInstallerCheck (sub_1C001F25C) or SmartlockerSmartscreenProcessTokenCheck (sub_1C001F374) → SmartlockerConstructOriginClaim (sub_1C001F138).
debugging_aids.expected_observation: At the return path (0x1C001F229), inspecting the buffer at rbx will show offsets 0x00-0x37 initialized (constants, random data, metadata), while bytes 0x3C-0x3F (when arg5==NULL) or the trailing bytes beyond the copied data (when arg5 is provided) contain uninitialized paged pool residue. The disclosed quantity is 4-6 bytes; the disassembly does not establish that the residue contains any particular class of sensitive data.
debugging_aids.notes: ExAllocatePoolWithTag does not zero memory. The tag 'smtA' (0x41746D73) identifies AppID Smartlocker pool allocations. The patched-only feature gate Feature_965997883__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage @ 0x1C0002194 reads the WIL feature state (checks bit 0x10 of Feature_965997883__private_featureState, otherwise calls the fallback) and controls whether the zeroing memset runs; when the feature is disabled the patched driver takes the original unzeroed allocation path. The buffer is placed at full length into a process-token security attribute and a file extended attribute (both user-mode-queryable via standard Windows APIs, with the read performed by the OS, not this driver).

security_findings.entry_point: WIL feature-enablement check (feature Feature_4060024122) inside appid.sys
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change (WIL feature-staging refactor; not CWE-362/CWE-366)
security_findings.description: This is NOT a security defect. wil_details_FeatureStateCache_TryEnableDeviceUsageFastPath (sub_1C00028DC) updates the WIL feature-enablement/usage state cache with a correct lock-free atomic compare-and-swap. In the unpatched build the state comes from the single global feature descriptor (wil_details_featureDescriptors_a, loaded at 0x1C00028DC); for event type 3 it sets bit 0x10, for event type 4 bit 0x20, using the loop: load current value (0x1C00028F9), or in the bit, lock cmpxchg (0x1C000290E), and on failure jnz (0x1C0002913) branches back to 0x1C00028FC to re-read and retry. That is a textbook CAS-with-retry, thread-safe with no torn read and no lost update, so there is no data race to fix. The patched build performs the same CAS on a per-descriptor context (*arg3) and adds a lock or fast path (0x1C0001C89); this is a WIL library refactor (global descriptor to per-descriptor context, a 2-arg to 3-arg ABI change), not a synchronization fix. None of these bits affect an AppLocker allow/deny decision.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Not applicable; there is no exploitable condition. Concurrent WIL feature-enablement checks for Feature_4060024122 reach wil_details_FeatureStateCache_TryEnableDeviceUsageFastPath via the thunk (sub_1C0002E00) and orchestrator (sub_1C0002850), and multiple threads may contend on the same global dword, but the lock-free CAS-with-retry handles contention correctly, so no inconsistent or torn state results.
security_findings.primitive: None. The atomic CAS is correct in both builds; the affected state is WIL feature enablement/usage telemetry, never an AppLocker allow/deny decision. There is no primitive and no policy bypass.
security_findings.assembly:
wil_details_FeatureStateCache_TryEnableDeviceUsageFastPath @ 0x1C00028DC (UNPATCHED):
00000001C00028DC  mov     r9, cs:wil_details_featureDescriptors_a  ; global descriptor
00000001C00028E3  sub     edx, 3
00000001C00028E6  jz      short loc_1C00028F3
00000001C00028E8  cmp     edx, 1
00000001C00028EB  jnz     short locret_1C0002915
00000001C00028ED  lea     r8d, [rdx+1Fh]                 ; 0x20 for event type 4
00000001C00028F1  jmp     short loc_1C00028F9
00000001C00028F3  mov     r8d, 10h                       ; 0x10 for event type 3
00000001C00028F9  mov     eax, [r9]                      ; load (re-read each retry)
00000001C00028FC  test    al, 2
00000001C00028FE  jz      short locret_1C0002915
00000001C0002900  mov     edx, eax
00000001C0002902  xor     edx, ecx
00000001C0002904  test    dl, 1
00000001C0002907  jnz     short locret_1C0002915
00000001C0002909  mov     edx, eax
00000001C000290B  or      edx, r8d
00000001C000290E  lock cmpxchg [r9], edx                 ; atomic CAS
00000001C0002913  jnz     short loc_1C00028FC             ; retry -> correct lock-free loop
00000001C0002915  retn

breakpoints.why: Function entry. cl=arg1 (parity/flags byte), edx=arg2 (event type 3 or 4). r9 is loaded from the global feature descriptor here.
The lock cmpxchg that atomically updates the state via r9. On failure the jnz at 0x1C0002913 re-reads and retries. This is a correct lock-free CAS: contention causes a retry, not corruption.

watch: r9 (at 0x1C00028DC) = pointer to the global feature descriptor state (unpatched)
cl (at entry) = arg1, bit 0 used as parity value for the CAS condition
edx (at entry) = arg2, event type: 3 sets bit 0x10, 4 sets bit 0x20
key_offsets: 0x1C00028DC: mov r9, cs:wil_details_featureDescriptors_a — loads the global descriptor pointer
0x1C00028F9: mov eax, [r9] — load current state (re-read on each retry)
0x1C0002913: jnz 0x1C00028FC — retry on CAS failure (re-reads current value; no lost update)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Not applicable; there is no exploitable race. For inspection, drive concurrent WIL feature-enablement checks for Feature_4060024122 so multiple threads reach wil_details_FeatureStateCache_TryEnableDeviceUsageFastPath (sub_1C00028DC).
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Under contention the cmpxchg at 0x1C000290E may fail and the jnz at 0x1C0002913 retries after re-reading the current value. This is the expected, correct behavior of a lock-free CAS: no update is lost and no state is torn or corrupted. Nothing security-relevant results, and no AppLocker allow/deny decision is involved.
debugging_aids.notes: In the unpatched build the state pointer is the global feature descriptor. In the patched build the third parameter arg3 supplies a per-descriptor context pointer, and the function also checks per-descriptor bytes at arg3+0x1d and arg3+0x1e to choose between a lock-or fast path and the full cmpxchg loop. This is a WIL library refactor (global descriptor to per-descriptor context), not a synchronization fix.

summary: The patch to the Windows AppID driver (appid.sys) contains one Low-severity security-relevant change and one non-security refactor. (1) Use of uninitialized resource (CWE-908, Low): SmartlockerConstructOriginClaim (sub_1C001F138) allocates a paged pool buffer via ExAllocatePoolWithTag without zeroing it, leaving 4 bytes uninitialized when its arg5 is NULL and roughly 6 trailing bytes otherwise; both callers pass the buffer at full length into a process-token security attribute (SeSetSecurityAttributesToken via SmartlockerTagProcessToken @ 0x1C001F0C0) and a file extended attribute (AipSetEaFromOrigin), which are user-mode-queryable via standard Windows APIs. The patched build adds a memset that zeroes the buffer, but only on the path taken when the WIL feature Feature_965997883 is enabled; the feature-disabled path retains the original unzeroed allocation, so the fix is a staged rollout. The disclosed quantity is small (4-6 bytes of pool residue) and the disassembly does not establish it carries any specific sensitive content, so KASLR-bypass and token-harvest claims are not made. (2) The wil_details_* feature-state cache functions (sub_1C00028DC, sub_1C0002850, sub_1C0002E00 and the two ReportUsage helpers) were refactored from a single global feature descriptor to a per-descriptor context passed as a parameter. The underlying atomic compare-and-swap is a correct lock-free CAS-with-retry in both builds, so there is no data race; none of these functions participate in an AppLocker allow/deny decision. This is WIL feature-staging library churn, not a security fix.
